summary: Kenneth reviews his weekly covered call performance on HIMS stock, highlighting a timing mistake where he sold a $50 call Monday morning for only $50 premium when the stock was down, versus waiting until Wednesday when he could have earned $130 on a similar strike. He also discusses a successful position where 200 shares were called away at $46, generating $392 total profit and freeing $9,000 in capital for reinvestment.
takeaways: Wait for stock recoveries before selling covered calls—RSI oversold + moving average crossovers + volume increase indicate better premium timing
Premium improvement example: $50 call sold at $46-47 stock price=$50 premium vs. waiting to $50 stock price=$130 premium (2.6x difference in 2-3 days)
Called away shares at $46 strike purchased at $45.87 = $26 profit on stock plus $366 in premiums = $392 total on 200-share position
Assignment frees capital for redeployment—200 shares called away freed ~$9,000 for next investment
Covered call strategy: sell calls slightly OTM, wait for intra-week stock strength to maximize premium rather than selling immediately on down days

strategy_candidates.change: Implement a rule: do not sell covered calls on down days at market open; wait 1-2 days for RSI oversold recovery + moving average recrossing before premium sale
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth's $46-47 sale for $50 premium vs. $50 stock price sale for $130 premium (same week, same strike)—waiting 2-3 days yielded 2.6x better premium on HIMS; RSI indicator and volume increase confirmed ideal entry timing Wednesday vs. Monday

title: PART 6.5 -- 7/11 - learn from my mistake
